mission.title: spire: Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE
mission.description: Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code in case that a profile is disabled is not defined by the specification. We need to address that at the specificatio`

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
There are some scenarios where JWT-SVID support is not needed, and servers could avoid having to maintain JWT authorities and the rotation of JWT keys.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in SPIRE
steps.description:
**Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**

Spire server JWT-SVID

**Description of change**

New option to disable JWT-SVID in spire server. JWT keys are no longer managed. Server and Agent api endpoints related to JWT return precondition failed grpc code

Answers from the issue:
- ret
```yaml
time="2025-08-26T13:35:22Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=transport method=MintJWTSVID request_id=5f535de6-d5e5-466a-beb4-ceab4f32e88f service=svid.v1.SVID subsystem_name=api
time="2025-08-26T13:35:22Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=transport method=PrepareJWTAuthority request_id=a756696c-c986-4e10-8f41-7c10a769f9e3 service=localauthority.v1.LocalAuthority subsystem_name=api
time="2025-08-26T13:35:23Z" level=error msg="JWT functionality is disabled" authorized_as=local authorized_via=tra
```

steps.title: Confirm Provide the ability to disable JWT-SVID support in is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code in case that a profile is disabled is not defined by the specification. We need to address that at the specificatio` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: **Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**

Spire server JWT-SVID

**Description of change**

New option to disable JWT-SVID in spire server. JWT keys are no longer managed. Server and Agent api endpoints related to JWT return precondition failed grpc code

Answers from the issue:
- returning FailedPrecondition grpc code in JWT related endpoints

- decided to go with disable_jwt so do. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
